project.name: Module 1: Project Scaffold
project.description: Configure the existing WXT + React + TypeScript project with all remaining configuration files, shared type definitions, CLAUDE.md updates, shadcn/ui initialization, Sentry with zero-PII beforeSend filter, and CI pipeline. This module produces the foundation every subsequent module builds on. No feature code is written here — only infrastructure, configuration, and conventions.
project.parent_plan: Hush Consolidated Build Plan — Day 1 (first half)

user_stories.title: Update CLAUDE.md with Progress Logging Protocol and Licensing Rules
user_stories.description: CLAUDE.md already exists with module boundary rules, crypto rules, Sentry rules, error handling, and import rules. This story adds the missing sections: Progress Logging Protocol (governing how .prd/ progress files are maintained) and Licensing & Architecture Rules (GPL-3.0, ProGate pattern, hush-sync separation). Also verify all existing content matches Build Plan conventions.
steps: RED: Create verify-claude-md.sh that greps CLAUDE.md for required phrases covering BOTH existing content AND new sections: 'Zero React/DOM imports' or 'zero DOM', 'empty catch' or 'no empty catch', 'PBKDF2', '600,000' or '600000', 'AES-256-GCM', 'beforeSend', 'Progress Logging Protocol', 'progress.md', 'NEVER overwrite previous entries', 'GPL-3.0', 'useProGate', 'hush-sync', 'Option B readiness'. Script exits non-zero if any phrase is missing. Run it — it should fail because Progress Logging Protocol and Licensing sections don't exist yet.

GREEN: Update CLAUDE.md — append Progress Logging Protocol section with rules: (1) Each module has a prd.json and a companion progress.md file in .prd/. (2) After completing each story, append a session entry to the module's progress.md — NEVER overwrite previous entries. (3) Each entry format: '## Session: {ISO timestamp}' + '**Task**: {story_id} - {story_title}' + '**Status**: PASSED or FAILED (attempt {n})'. (4) Required subsections: '### Work Done' (bullet points), '### Files Created' (table: File | Purpose), '### Files Modified' (table: File | Changes), '### Acceptance Criteria Verification' (numbered checklist with ✅/❌), '### Verification Results' (exact command output). (5) If FAILED, add '### Failure Analysis' with root cause before retrying. (6) After appending to progress.md, update prd.json: set story's 'passes' to true, increment 'attempt_count', update 'metadata.passing_stories'. (7) Update Story Tracker table at top of progress.md. (8) After ALL stories complete, append '## Module Summary'. (9) Reference progress_log.format_rules in each prd.json for authoritative rules.

Append Licensing & Architecture Rules section: (1) The hush repo is GPL-3.0. Every file is GPL-3.0. (2) Holy Private Bookmarks attribution preserved in extracted files (crypto.ts, data-model.ts). (3) Pro features are GPL-3.0 but gated by ExtensionPay license checks via useProGate() hook. (4) All Pro-gated features keep business logic in lib/ (not components) for Option B readiness. (5) The sync backend (hush-sync) is a SEPARATE proprietary repo. Never import sync backend code into hush. (6) lib/sync-client.ts talks to backend via HTTPS API only — zero backend logic in the extension.
Run verify-claude-md.sh — should now pass. Delete the script afterwards.
REFACTOR: Verify every rule traces back to Build Plan Section 3 or 4
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md exists at project root (already true — verify unchanged)
Contains module boundary rules (lib/ = no DOM, components = no chrome.storage) — already present
Contains error handling convention (no empty catch blocks) — already present
Contains crypto rules (PBKDF2 iterations, no ECB, no hardcoded IVs) — already present
Contains React + shadcn rules (functional only, no Redux) — already present
Contains Sentry rules (beforeSend strips URLs and bookmark titles) — already present
Contains line count targets for all major files — already present
Contains import rules (WXT browser global, type imports) — already present
NEW: Contains Progress Logging Protocol section with all 9 rules
NEW: Contains Licensing & Architecture Rules section with GPL-3.0, ProGate pattern, Option B readiness, hush-sync separation
definition_of_done: verify-claude-md.sh passes (all key phrases present including new sections)
Every rule traces back to Build Plan Section 3 or 4
Progress Logging Protocol section is complete with all 9 rules
Licensing & Architecture Rules section is complete

user_stories.title: Configure WXT project with manifest permissions and clean boilerplate
user_stories.description: The WXT project is already initialized with React module, all dependencies installed, and entrypoints scaffolded. This story configures wxt.config.ts with correct manifest permissions (storage, contextMenus, activeTab, history, bookmarks — NO clipboardRead/Write), renames the package, cleans up template boilerplate, and removes the unused content script.
steps: RED: Write tests/unit/config/wxt-config.test.ts that imports or reads wxt.config.ts output and asserts: permissions include 'storage', 'contextMenus', 'activeTab', 'history', 'bookmarks'; permissions do NOT include 'clipboardRead' or 'clipboardWrite'; the built manifest has the correct name and description
GREEN: Update wxt.config.ts — add manifest.permissions array, set manifest.name to 'Hush Private Bookmarks', set manifest.description, configure @wxt-dev/module-react (already present)
Update package.json — change name from 'wxt-react-starter' to 'hush-private-bookmarks', update version to '0.1.0', update description
Clean up entrypoints/popup/App.tsx — remove counter demo, WXT/React logos, replace with 'Hush' placeholder text
Remove entrypoints/popup/App.css (template styles)
Clean up entrypoints/popup/style.css — remove template styles, prepare for Tailwind replacement in SCAFFOLD-003
Remove entrypoints/content.ts (not needed for Hush — no content script injection)
Update entrypoints/background.ts — remove console.log, add empty onInstalled handler
Run `wxt build` and verify it produces a valid extension package
Run config test to verify permissions
acceptance_criteria: wxt.config.ts declares permissions: storage, contextMenus, activeTab, history, bookmarks
wxt.config.ts does NOT declare clipboardRead or clipboardWrite permissions
wxt.config.ts integrates @wxt-dev/module-react (already true)
package.json name is 'hush-private-bookmarks'
`wxt build` completes without errors
Extension loads in Chrome as unpacked and popup renders 'Hush' text
entrypoints/background.ts uses WXT defineBackground() pattern with no console.log
entrypoints/content.ts is removed
Template boilerplate (counter, logos) is removed from popup
Config validation test passes
definition_of_done: Extension loads in chrome://extensions as unpacked
user_stories.verification: wxt build && npx vitest run tests/unit/config/wxt-config.test.ts

user_stories.title: Strict TypeScript configuration (tsconfig.json)
user_stories.description: Enhance tsconfig.json with additional strict flags beyond what WXT provides. WXT's generated .wxt/tsconfig.json already sets strict: true and provides @/* path aliases. This story adds noUncheckedIndexedAccess (critical for safe array/object access), noUnusedLocals, and noUnusedParameters.
steps: RED: Write tests/unit/config/tsconfig-validation.test.ts — behavioral test that verifies: (1) create a temp .ts file with implicit any usage and verify tsc catches it, (2) verify @/lib/* imports resolve (create a minimal lib/types.ts stub if needed for the test)

GREEN: Update tsconfig.json — add to compilerOptions: noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, noUnusedLocals: true, noUnusedParameters: true. Keep existing: extends .wxt/tsconfig.json, jsx: react-jsx, allowImportingTsExtensions: true
Run `tsc --noEmit` and verify zero errors on existing entrypoint files
REFACTOR: Verify .wxt/tsconfig.json strict settings are not weakened by our overrides
acceptance_criteria: tsconfig.json has noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true
tsconfig.json has noUnusedLocals: true and noUnusedParameters: true
tsconfig.json extends .wxt/tsconfig.json (already true)
strict: true inherited from .wxt/tsconfig.json (already true)
Path aliases @/* inherited from .wxt/tsconfig.json (already true)
`tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors

user_stories.title: Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui initialization with dark mode
user_stories.description: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 (already installed as @tailwindcss/vite plugin) with shadcn/ui for component primitives. Tailwind v4 uses CSS-based configuration — no tailwind.config.ts needed. Dark mode via class strategy in CSS. Initialize shadcn/ui (which auto-detects Tailwind v4) and add base components: Dialog, Input, Button, Alert.
steps: RED: Write tests/unit/config/styling-setup.test.ts that verifies: (1) globals.css exists and contains '@import "tailwindcss"', (2) components/ui/ directory contains button.tsx, input.tsx, dialog.tsx, alert.tsx files

GREEN: Create app.css (or globals.css) with Tailwind v4 setup: '@import "tailwindcss"' directive, CSS custom properties for shadcn/ui theming (light and dark themes using OKLCH colors), dark mode class variant
Run `npx shadcn@latest init` — the CLI detects Tailwind v4 and generates components.json with correct configuration. If prompted: style=default, base-color=neutral, css-variables=yes
Run `npx shadcn@latest add dialog input button alert` to install base components
Verify components/ui/ directory contains dialog.tsx, input.tsx, button.tsx, alert.tsx
Update entrypoints/popup/main.tsx to import the global CSS file
Update entrypoints/popup/App.tsx to render a shadcn Button with Tailwind classes to verify integration
Ensure @tailwindcss/vite is registered in wxt.config.ts vite plugins (may already be handled by WXT)
Run `wxt build` and verify popup renders styled button
REFACTOR: Verify shadcn components use @/components/ui path alias correctly
acceptance_criteria: Global CSS file uses Tailwind v4 '@import "tailwindcss"' (NOT v3 @tailwind directives)
No tailwind.config.ts file exists (v4 uses CSS-based config)
Dark mode configured via CSS class strategy
shadcn/ui CSS variables defined in global CSS using OKLCH color format
components/ui/ contains Dialog, Input, Button, Alert
Popup renders a styled shadcn Button component
No shadcn primitives were modified after installation (pristine copies)
lib/utils.ts created by shadcn init (cn() utility)
definition_of_done: `wxt build` passes with Tailwind v4 and shadcn integrated
Popup shows styled button when loaded as unpacked extension
No tailwind.config.ts exists in project root
user_stories.verification: wxt build && tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run tests/unit/config/styling-setup.test.ts

user_stories.title: Vitest configuration with coverage thresholds
user_stories.description: Configure Vitest (already installed) with v8 coverage provider, jsdom environment for component tests, and enforcement thresholds: 80% branch coverage for lib/, 60% for entrypoints/. The test runner must work with WXT's TypeScript setup and path aliases.
steps: RED: Write tests/unit/config/vitest-config.test.ts — behavioral test that verifies: (1) vitest can resolve @/lib/* imports, (2) coverage reporter runs and produces output

GREEN: Create vitest.config.ts with: coverage.provider = 'v8', environment = 'jsdom', coverage thresholds for lib/ (branches: 80, functions: 80, lines: 80, statements: 80) and entrypoints/ (branches: 60, functions: 60, lines: 60, statements: 60)
Configure resolve.alias to match WXT path aliases (@/* -> project root)
Write tests/unit/sanity.test.ts (assert 1 + 1 === 2) to verify runner works
Run `npx vitest run` and verify the sanity test passes with coverage output
acceptance_criteria: vitest.config.ts uses v8 coverage provider
jsdom environment configured for component tests
Coverage thresholds: 80% for lib/, 60% for entrypoints/
Path aliases resolve in test files (@/lib, @/components)
`npx vitest run` executes and reports coverage
Sanity test passes
definition_of_done: `npx vitest run` exits 0 with coverage report
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run && npx vitest run tests/unit/config/vitest-config.test.ts

user_stories.title: Playwright E2E configuration with extension loading fixture
user_stories.description: Configure Playwright (already installed) for browser extension E2E testing. Create a custom fixture that loads the built extension via --load-extension flag in Chromium. Tests directory: tests/e2e/.
steps: RED: Write tests/e2e/sanity.test.ts that uses the extension fixture to load the built extension and asserts the popup page contains 'Hush' text
GREEN: Create playwright.config.ts with: testDir: 'tests/e2e', Chromium project only (extensions require new headless mode), timeout: 30s, retries: 1 in CI

Create tests/e2e/fixtures/extension.ts with a custom fixture that: runs `wxt build` if needed, launches Chromium with --disable-extensions-except and --load-extension pointing to .output/chrome-mv3, exposes an extensionId fixture for navigating to chrome-extension://[id]/popup.html
Run `npx playwright install chromium` to ensure browser binary is available
Run `npx playwright test` and verify the sanity test loads the extension popup

Custom fixture loads built extension via --load-extension flag
Fixture exposes extensionId for navigating to popup/manager pages
Sanity test navigates to popup and finds expected content
Tests run in CI-compatible mode (new headless with extension support)
definition_of_done: `npx playwright test` exits 0 with sanity test passing
Extension popup loads in Playwright-controlled browser
Fixture properly sets up and tears down browser context

user_stories.title: ESLint v10 flat config enforcing project conventions
user_stories.description: Configure ESLint v10 (already installed) with flat config format (eslint.config.js). Rules enforce Build Plan conventions: no empty catch blocks, no console.log in production, prefer-const, explicit-function-return-type. ESLint v10 uses flat config exclusively — .eslintrc.json is not supported.
steps: RED: Write tests/unit/config/eslint-config.test.ts — behavioral test that: (1) reads eslint.config.js and verifies it exports an array, (2) verifies the config includes typescript-eslint plugin

GREEN: Create eslint.config.js using flat config format: import tseslint from '@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin', configure rules: no-empty: error, no-console: warn, prefer-const: error, @typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type: warn, @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars: error
Configure ignores array for: .wxt/, .output/, node_modules/, .prd/, coverage/, test-results/
Run `npx eslint .` on existing files and fix any violations
Add scripts to package.json: 'lint': 'eslint .'
REFACTOR: Ensure ESLint config is compatible with WXT-generated files in .wxt/
acceptance_criteria: eslint.config.js exists (NOT .eslintrc.json — ESLint v10 flat config only)
no-empty rule is set to 'error' (catches silent catch blocks)
no-console rule is set to 'warn' (flags console.log in production code)
prefer-const rule is set to 'error'
explicit-function-return-type is configured
ESLint runs cleanly on all existing files
.wxt/, .output/, .prd/ directories are excluded via ignores
definition_of_done: `npx eslint .` exits 0 (no --ext flag needed in v10)
user_stories.verification: npx eslint . && npx vitest run tests/unit/config/eslint-config.test.ts

user_stories.title: Shared type definitions (lib/types.ts)
user_stories.description: Create lib/types.ts with all shared type definitions that form the contract between modules. These types must match the Holy PB data format exactly for backward compatibility. Types include: Bookmark, Folder, BookmarkTree, EncryptedStore, PasswordSet, RecoveryPhrase, CryptoConfig, StorageError, and Result<T, E> utility type.
steps: RED: Write tests/unit/lib/types.test.ts that verifies type definitions compile correctly by creating valid instances of each type and asserting TypeScript accepts them. Test that invalid shapes are rejected (use @ts-expect-error annotations for negative tests)

GREEN: Create lib/types.ts with interfaces: Bookmark { type: 'bookmark'; title: string; url: string; dateAdded: number; id: string }, Folder { type: 'folder'; name: string; children: BookmarkNode[]; dateAdded: number; id: string }, BookmarkNode (Bookmark | Folder discriminated union), BookmarkTree (root Folder), EncryptedStore { salt: string; encrypted: string; iv: string; iterations: number }, PasswordSet { id: string; name: string; store: EncryptedStore; createdAt: number }, RecoveryPhrase { words: string[]; derivedKeyHash: string }, CryptoConfig { iterations: number; algorithm: string; keyLength: number }
Create error types in lib/errors.ts: DecryptionError, InvalidPasswordError, StorageError (with context field), ImportError — all extending Error
Create Result<T, E> utility type: { success: true; data: T } | { success: false; error: E }
Ensure all types use 'type' keyword for imports (import type { Bookmark } from '@/lib/types')
Run `tsc --noEmit` to verify all types compile
REFACTOR: Add JSDoc comments to each interface explaining its role and which Holy PB structure it maps to
acceptance_criteria: Bookmark interface has type: 'bookmark', title, url, dateAdded, id fields
Folder interface has type: 'folder', name, children (BookmarkNode[]), dateAdded, id fields
BookmarkNode is a discriminated union of Bookmark | Folder
EncryptedStore has salt, encrypted, iv, iterations fields matching Holy PB format
PasswordSet interface supports multiple independent encrypted collections
RecoveryPhrase interface supports BIP39 mnemonic data
Error types in lib/errors.ts: DecryptionError, InvalidPasswordError, StorageError, ImportError all extend Error with context
Result<T, E> utility type provides type-safe success/failure handling
All types compile under strict TypeScript with noUncheckedIndexedAccess
definition_of_done: Type validation test passes (both positive and negative cases)
No circular imports between lib/ modules
user_stories.verification: tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/types.test.ts && npx eslint lib/

user_stories.title: Sentry initialization with zero-PII beforeSend filter
user_stories.description: Configure @sentry/browser (already installed) with Sentry initialization for both background.ts and popup entry. The critical requirement: beforeSend MUST strip URLs and bookmark titles from all error reports. No breadcrumbs. No session replay. DSN injected at build time via import.meta.env.WXT_SENTRY_DSN.
steps: RED: Write tests/unit/lib/sentry-config.test.ts that imports the Sentry initialization module and asserts: beforeSend function exists, beforeSend strips URLs from error events (test with a mock error containing a URL), beforeSend strips bookmark titles from error context, breadcrumbs are disabled, session replay is disabled

GREEN: Create lib/sentry.ts with initSentry() function that calls Sentry.init() with: dsn from import.meta.env.WXT_SENTRY_DSN (build-time injection — WXT exposes WXT_-prefixed env vars to client code), beforeSend that strips request.url, strips any string matching URL patterns from extra context, strips bookmark-related fields. Set integrations to exclude BrowserTracing breadcrumbs and session replay
Create .env.example with WXT_SENTRY_DSN=your-dsn-here as documentation for contributors
Update entrypoints/background.ts to call initSentry() in defineBackground setup
Update entrypoints/popup/main.tsx to call initSentry() before React root render
Run Sentry config tests to verify PII stripping works
REFACTOR: Extract beforeSend filter to a separate pure function for testability
acceptance_criteria: lib/sentry.ts exports initSentry() function
beforeSend strips URLs from error events (request.url, breadcrumb URLs)
beforeSend strips bookmark titles from error extra/context fields
Breadcrumbs are disabled (no navigation or click tracking)
Session replay is NOT included
DSN loaded via import.meta.env.WXT_SENTRY_DSN (build-time injection)
initSentry() called in both background.ts and popup main.tsx
beforeSend filter is a pure function with its own unit tests
.env.example documents the WXT_SENTRY_DSN variable
definition_of_done: beforeSend correctly strips URLs (verified by test with mock error event)
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/sentry-config.test.ts && tsc --noEmit && npx eslint lib/sentry.ts

user_stories.title: Directory structure + .gitignore + i18n locales + icons + licensing files

Create icon placeholders. Update .gitignore for coverage and Playwright results. Create React Error Boundary shell component. Verify GPL-3.0 LICENSE exists. Create NOTICE file with Holy Private Bookmarks attribution.

GREEN: Create directories with .gitkeep files where needed: hooks/, public/icons/, tests/e2e/, .prd/module-01-scaffold/

Clone Holy PB to /tmp/holy-pb-reference if not already cloned. Copy src/_locales/ (NOT root _locales/) to public/_locales/ — 10 locales: ar, de, en, es, fr, hy, it, ru, uk, zh_CN. Each contains messages.json.
Create public/icons/ with placeholder PNG files (16x16, 48x48, 128x128)

acceptance_criteria: All directories from architecture diagram exist (lib/, components/ui/, hooks/, tests/)
public/_locales/ contains 10 locale directories: ar, de, en, es, fr, hy, it, ru, uk, zh_CN
public/icons/ has placeholder icon files at 3 sizes
ErrorBoundary.tsx catches errors, renders fallback UI, captures to Sentry
.gitignore excludes .output/, .wxt/, node_modules/, coverage/, test-results/
No generated files tracked in git

NOTICE file attributes Holy Private Bookmarks with repo URL and derived file list

`wxt build` still succeeds with new directory structure

user_stories.title: GitHub Actions CI pipeline
user_stories.description: Create the CI pipeline that runs on every push: tsc --noEmit, eslint, vitest --coverage, playwright test. Nightly builds against Chrome Canary, Firefox Nightly, Edge Dev. This is the automated quality gate — no code merges without passing CI.
steps: RED: Validate workflow YAML structure is correct (use actionlint if available)

GREEN: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with: trigger on push and pull_request to main, job 'typecheck' (tsc --noEmit), job 'lint' (npx eslint .), job 'unit-tests' (vitest run --coverage), job 'e2e-tests' (wxt build, playwright test), all jobs on ubuntu-latest with Node.js 20
Create .github/workflows/nightly.yml with: cron trigger (daily at 3am UTC), jobs that build extension for Chrome Canary, Firefox Nightly, Edge Dev channels using WXT browser targets
Add caching for node_modules (actions/cache with package-lock.json hash)
Upload coverage reports and Playwright results as artifacts
E2E job needs xvfb-run for headless Chromium with extension support in CI
Add branch protection rules reminder in CLAUDE.md (require CI pass before merge)
acceptance_criteria: CI triggers on push and pull_request to main
CI runs tsc, eslint (flat config, no --ext), vitest, playwright in sequence
Node.js 20 used across all jobs
node_modules cached with package-lock.json hash
Coverage and Playwright artifacts uploaded
E2E tests use xvfb-run for CI headless support
Nightly workflow builds for Chrome Canary, Firefox Nightly, Edge Dev
Nightly runs on cron schedule
definition_of_done: CI YAML is valid (actionlint or act --dryrun passes)
Both workflows reference correct Node.js version and caching strategy
CI pipeline would pass if pushed (all existing tests pass locally)
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run && tsc --noEmit && npx eslint .

user_stories.title: Full scaffold integration verification
user_stories.description: End-to-end verification that all scaffold pieces work together. Build the extension, run all tests (unit + e2e), verify coverage reporting, verify lint passes, verify the extension loads and popup renders with styled shadcn components. This is the quality gate for Day 1 first half.
steps: RED: Write tests/unit/integration/scaffold-smoke.test.ts that verifies: lib/types.ts can be imported, lib/errors.ts can be imported, lib/sentry.ts can be imported, components/ErrorBoundary.tsx can be imported, all imports resolve via @/* path aliases
GREEN: Fix any integration issues surfaced by the smoke test (path resolution, circular dependencies, missing exports)
Run the full verification sequence: tsc --noEmit → eslint . → vitest run --coverage → wxt build → playwright test
Verify bundle size: wxt build output should be well under 200KB gzipped (scaffold only, minimal code)
Verify coverage report generates correctly (even if thresholds aren't met yet — lib/ has minimal code)
REFACTOR: Create package.json scripts: 'verify': 'tsc --noEmit && eslint . && vitest run && wxt build', 'test:e2e': 'playwright test'
acceptance_criteria: tsc --noEmit passes (zero type errors)
ESLint passes (zero errors) using flat config
All Vitest tests pass
wxt build produces valid extension package
Playwright E2E sanity test passes
All @/* path aliases resolve correctly across test and source files
package.json has 'verify' and 'test:e2e' scripts
definition_of_done: Extension loads in Chrome and popup renders styled content
Ready to hand off to Module 2 (Crypto)
user_stories.verification: npm run verify && npx playwright test tests/e2e/sanity.test.ts

metadata.session_protocol: One Claude Code session for scaffold. Review all config files. Run tsc --noEmit. Run vitest. Verify extension loads. Commit.
metadata.quality_gate: All config tests pass. tsc clean. wxt build succeeds. Extension loads in Chrome. shadcn/ui components render in popup shell.

format_rules: Append a new session entry after completing each story (do NOT overwrite previous entries)
Each entry starts with `## Session: {ISO timestamp}` followed by `**Task**: {story_id} - {story_title}`
Include ### Work Done section with bullet points describing what was implemented
Include ### Files Created table (File | Purpose) for any new files
Include ### Files Modified table (File | Changes) for any changed files
Include ### Acceptance Criteria Verification with numbered checklist using ✅ or ❌
Include ### Verification Results showing exact command output (tsc, vitest, eslint, etc.)
If story FAILED, include ### Failure Analysis with root cause before retrying
After all stories complete, append a ## Module Summary with total pass/fail counts and any notes for the next module
Update the story's `passes` field to true and increment `attempt_count` in the prd.json after each attempt
Update `metadata.passing_stories` count in the prd.json after each story passes

starting_state.description: The repo is already initialized from the WXT React template. All npm dependencies are installed. CLAUDE.md exists with most conventions. Entrypoint stubs exist with template boilerplate. No lib/, hooks/, or test files exist yet. No ESLint, Vitest, or Playwright configs exist yet.
files_that_exist: wxt.config.ts (minimal — only enables @wxt-dev/module-react, no manifest config)
CLAUDE.md (comprehensive — missing Progress Logging Protocol and Licensing sections)
entrypoints/popup/App.tsx (template counter demo with WXT/React logos)
entrypoints/content.ts (content script — not needed, to be removed)

external_dependencies.name: Holy Private Bookmarks
external_dependencies.clone_instructions: Clone to /tmp/holy-pb-reference. READ-ONLY reference — do NOT build Hush inside this clone.

files_to_extract.src/_locales/: Copy entire directory to public/_locales/ in SCAFFOLD-010. 10 locales: ar, de, en, es, fr, hy, it, ru, uk, zh_CN. Source path is src/_locales/ (NOT root _locales/).
files_to_extract.js/crypto-wrapper.js: Reference for Module 2 (crypto.ts). 174 lines. PBKDF2 600K + AES-256-GCM. Do NOT extract in this module.
files_to_extract.js/shared.js: Reference for Module 4 (data-model.ts). 888 lines. Do NOT extract in this module.

tech_stack.styling: Tailwind CSS v4.2.1 with @tailwindcss/vite plugin (CSS-based config, NOT tailwind.config.ts) + shadcn/ui

licensing.extension_license: GPL-3.0. Every file in the hush repo is GPL-3.0.
licensing.attribution: NOTICE file at project root attributing Holy Private Bookmarks (github.com/OSV-IT-Studio/holy-private-bookmarks, GPL-3.0). Derived files: crypto.ts, data-model.ts, _locales/.
licensing.pro_features: Pro features are GPL-3.0 but gated by ExtensionPay license checks. Code is open source; functionality is gated.
licensing.sync_backend: The sync backend (hush-sync) is a SEPARATE proprietary repo. Not part of this module.
licensing.pro_gate_pattern: All Pro-gated features use lib/pro-gate.ts with useProGate() hook. Introduced in Module 16a.

read_first: This PRD file (read all stories and dependencies before starting)
.wxt/tsconfig.json (understand what WXT provides before adding tsconfig overrides)

outputs.description: Updated with Progress Logging Protocol and Licensing Rules

outputs.consumed_by: Module 2 (Crypto), Module 3 (Storage), Module 4 (Data Model), all subsequent
All modules that add entrypoints or permissions
outputs.description: Configured with manifest permissions and React module
Vitest with coverage thresholds and path aliases

outputs.consumed_by: All modules that write E2E tests

outputs.description: ESLint v10 flat config with project conventions
Sentry init with zero-PII beforeSend filter

mandatory: Read this ENTIRE prd.json before starting any story
Follow the critical_path dependency order — never start a story before its depends_on stories pass
Run the verification command listed in each story after completing it
Append to .prd/module-01-scaffold/progress.md after each story (follow progress_log.format_rules exactly)
Update this prd.json after each story: set passes=true, increment attempt_count, update metadata.passing_stories
Clone Holy Private Bookmarks to /tmp/holy-pb-reference ONCE at the start — do NOT re-clone for each story
Do NOT install npm packages that are already in package.json — all dependencies are pre-installed
forbidden: Do NOT run npx wxt@latest init — the project is already initialized
Do NOT create tailwind.config.ts — Tailwind v4 uses CSS-based configuration
Do NOT create .eslintrc.json — ESLint v10 uses flat config (eslint.config.js) exclusively
Do NOT use @tailwind base/components/utilities directives — Tailwind v4 uses @import 'tailwindcss'
Do NOT use eslint --ext flag — removed in ESLint v10
Do NOT modify files in .wxt/ — these are auto-generated by WXT
Do NOT build the Hush extension inside the Holy PB reference clone
Do NOT add clipboardRead or clipboardWrite permissions to the manifest
